learner_goal: I want to explain why ice cream sales and drowning both rise in summer without one causing the other.
launch_attempt: Two things can move together because a third factor affects both, not because one causes the other.
cold_attempt: I'm not sure — does more ice cream cause more drowning?
Heat is a confounder: summer drives swimming and ice cream separately, so correlation does not imply causation.
repair_dialogue_turns: The preview helps because both go up in summer.
Hot weather is the confounder: summer heat raises swimming and ice cream demand independently, so the correlation does not imply ice cream causes drowning.
spaced_attempt: Ice cream sales and drowning correlate in summer because heat is a confounder: it increases swimming exposure and ice cream consumption separately. Correlation alone does not establish causation without ruling out confounds and showing a direct mechanism.
